reason: Load backend project rules before implementing M3 eval modules and CLI integration.
M3 adds a new eval package and tests; module boundaries should follow backend structure conventions.
M3 is primarily a quality gate; implementation must emphasize deterministic tests and regression coverage.
Eval CLI loader and runner errors should be clear, structured, and testable without breaking API error contracts.
M3 data flow crosses fixtures, config, CLI, build/load state, wave search, and reports.
Reuse existing build_kb/load_kb/wave_search/Result contracts rather than duplicating retrieval logic.
Use original roadmap and M0 quality NFRs for precision@k/MRR thresholds and eval scope.
M3 should respect M2 multi-KB behavior while keeping default eval offline and independent of auth/cache/rate-limit.
